src/conversation/indexer.ts.note: VERIFIED BUG (2026-06-10, empirical /tmp harness): the legacy-offset upgrade probe (isRewoundLastTurn, ~lines 65-71) compares ONLY userText. With a legacy EOF-semantics offset AND a new turn whose user text equals the stored last turn's (common: "continue"), the probe mis-binds — the new turn REPLACES the stored last turn instead of appending. One turn is lost and session tokens undercount. A from-0 re-parse fully recovers (deleteTurnsAbove reconciliation works). All other paths verified clean: mid-turn splits at every line boundary, gapped legacy indices, tool-result-only continuations, foreign-cwd interleave, mid-pass appends; embed churn bounded. Fix direction: strengthen the probe (also require stored assistantText to be a prefix of the re-parsed turn's, or persist/compare the last turn's startByteOffset).
src/conversation/indexer.ts.author: agent

src/embeddings/embed.ts.note: Do NOT raise this default for throughput. History: shipped at cores/2, users complained indexing made machines unusable while working; lowered to cores/3 deliberately. Benchmarks WILL show 1.5-2x index speedup at 6-8 threads (measured 2026-06-10: 2t=19.4ms/chunk, 6t=9.4, 8t=9.0 on 10-core M-series) — that's the trap. Day-to-day machine impact wins over index speed for the default; per-project config.indexThreads is the escape hatch for users who want a fast foreground index.
src/embeddings/embed.ts.author: agent

benchmarks/contextbench/graph-completene.note: call✓ metric (resolved/linkable symbol refs) is COLLISION-INFLATED — do not read it as "call graph 70% incomplete". Drill-down (callgraph-miss-probe.ts on matplotlib): ~99% of unresolved-linkable refs are NO_IMPORT (file binds no import of that name); only ~1% are import-cascade misses. Of NO_IMPORT, ~half are ambiguous common names and the "unique-exporter" half is ALSO mostly noise: local vars / builtins (line, item, type, object, path, range) and method/attribute dispatch (ax.plot, fig.legend → matches pyplot.plot export) that share a name with one project export. Resolving those needs receiver TYPE INFERENCE, which mimirs deliberately won't do (static, zero-LLM). Conclusion: the resolver already captures ~all statically-recoverable edges; call-graph "incompleteness" is not a fixable gap. Import graph (~98%) is the real asset — lean retrieval levers on imports, not the symbol/call graph (explains why callee-expand-lab was weak).
benchmarks/contextbench/graph-completene.author: agent

src/search/hybrid.ts.note: Twin scoring bug (still UNFIXED here, fixed in searchChunks' inline affinity 2026-06-07): (1) stemWords = stem.split(/[_-]+/) has no length guard, so a stem like "__init__" -> ["","init",""] and the empty string matches EVERY query word via qw.includes("") -> stemMatchCount explodes the multiplier (saw 129x on a long query, rocketing __init__.py to rank 1). (2) queryWords not deduped -> filename affinity inflated by term frequency. Effect scales with query length: catastrophic for long issue text (ContextBench), mild for short topic queries (why internal recall@K didn't visibly break). Fix mirrors searchChunks: stemWords.filter(w=>w.length>=3) + [...new Set(queryWords)]. Left unfixed because this feeds file-level search() (internal recall@K benchmark), needs re-verification there — out of scope for the ContextBench-only scoring goal.
src/search/hybrid.ts.author: agent
